zh.lifeSummary: 當James Grosvenor於大約1447年出生在英國爾伊頓時，他的父親Ralph Grosvenor41歲，母親Joan Eaton32歲。他於大約1459年與Margaret Stanley在威爾士福林爾結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当James Grosvenor于大约1447年出生在英国爾伊頓时，他的父亲Ralph Grosvenor41岁，母亲Joan Eaton32岁。他于大约1459年与Margaret Stanley在威尔士福林爾结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Grosvenor
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from French gros ‘big, great’ + veneur ‘hunter’.

History: This is the name of one of the wealthiest families in Britain, which holds the title Duke of Westminster. They have been long established in Cheshire, with strong links with the city of Chester. One of the earliest recorded bearers of the name was Robert le Grosvenor of Budworth, who was granted lands by the Earl of Chester in 1160. The family's fortunes were founded by Thomas Grosvenor (born 1656), who in 1677 married an heiress, Mary Davies, whose inheritance included Ebury Farm, Middlesex. This now forms an area of central London that includes Grosvenor Square and Belgrave Square.

variants.name: Gravenor
meanings: English (of Norman origin): nickname from Old French grand ‘big, tall’ + veneor ‘hunter’. Compare Grosvenor .

nameMeanings.name: James
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name borne in the New Testament by two of Christ's disciples, James son of Zebedee and James son of Alphaeus. This form comes from Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Iacobus, Latin form of Greek Iakobos. This is the same name as Old Testament Jacob (Hebrew Yaakov), but for many centuries now they have been thought of in the English-speaking world as two distinct names. In Britain, James is a royal name that from the beginning of the 15th century onwards was associated particularly with the Scottish house of Stewart: James I of Scotland ( 1394–1437 ; ruled 1424–37 ) was a patron of the arts and a noted poet, as well as an energetic ruler. King James VI of Scotland ( 1566–1625 ; reigned 1567–1625 ) succeeded to the throne of England in 1603 . His grandson, James II of England ( 1633–1701 ; reigned 1685–8 ) was a Roman Catholic, deposed in 1688 in favour of his Protestant daughter Mary and her husband William of Orange. From then on he, his son (also called James), and his grandson Charles (‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’) made various unsuccessful attempts to recover the English throne. Their supporters were known as Jacobites (from Latin Iacobus), and the name James became for a while particularly associated with Roman Catholicism on the one hand, and Highland opposition to the English government on the other. Nevertheless, it has since become one of the most perennially popular boys' names.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When James Grosvenor was born about 1447, in Eaton, Cheshire, England, his father, Ralph Grosvenor, was 41 and his mother, Joan Eaton, was 32. He married Margaret Stanley about 1459, in Flintshire, Wales. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque James Grosvenor est né environ 1447, à Eaton, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, Ralph Grosvenor, avait 41 ans et sa mère, Joan Eaton, avait 32 ans. Il a épousé Margaret Stanley environ 1459, à Flintshire, Galles. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille.

de.lifeSummary: Als James Grosvenor um 1447 in Eaton, Cheshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Ralph Grosvenor 41 und seine Mutter Joan Eaton 32. Er hat um 1459 in Flintshire, Wales Margaret Stanley geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Quando James Grosvenor è nato il circa 1447 nel Eaton, Cheshire, England, il suo padre, Ralph Grosvenor, aveva 41 anni e il suo madre, Joan Eaton, aveva 32 anni. Lui sposato Margaret Stanley circa 1459, nel Flintshire, Galles. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia.

ja.Flintshire, Wales: ウェールズフリントシュアイア

ja.lifeSummary: James Grosvenorが約1447年にイギリスチェシュアイアエアトンで生まれた時，父Ralph Grosvenorは41歳，母Joan Eatonは32歳であった。約1459年にウェールズフリントシュアイアでMargaret Stanleyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: James Grosvenor(이)가 대략 1447년 영국 체실르 이턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Ralph Grosvenor(은)는 41세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan Eaton(은)는 32세였다. 그는 대략 1459년 웨일스 플린츠히르에서 Margaret Stanley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando James Grosvenor nasceu aproximadamente 1447, em Eaton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Ralph Grosvenor, tinha 41 anos e sua mãe, Joan Eaton, tinha 32 anos. Ele casou-se com Margaret Stanley aproximadamente 1459, em Flintshire, Gales. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда James Grosvenor родился около 1447 г. в местности эатон, чешир, Англия, его отцу по имени Ralph Grosvenor было 41 год, а матери, которую звали Joan Eaton, 32. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret Stanley около 1459 г. в местности флинцхир, Уэльс. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando James Grosvenor nació aproximadamente 1447, en Eaton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Ralph Grosvenor, tenía 41 años y su mamá, Joan Eaton, tenía 32 años. Se casó con Margaret Stanley aproximadamente 1459, en Flintshire, Gales. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija.
